title: 5 best new restaurant openings and dining updates in Boston (April 9, 2026)
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Beyond Proof, Boston's first zero-proof bar, opens in Jamaica Plain offering Mediterranean and Middle Eastern dishes.
Mezo Mediterranean, a fast-fine dining venue from Dedham House of Pizza, opens in Dedham Square serving Greek cuisine.
Island Creek Oyster raw bar pop-up returns to the Seaport late April with oysters, caviar, tacos, and frozen cocktails.

title: Rachael Rollins, former U.S. Attorney who resigned amid ethics scandal, reportedly running again for Suffolk DA
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Rachael Rollins, former U.S. Attorney, filed to run for Suffolk County District Attorney, her previous role before 2021.
Rollins faces incumbent DA Kevin Hayden and attorney Linda Champion in the upcoming Suffolk District Attorney election.
Rollins resigned as U.S. Attorney in 2023 amid ethics scandals, including interfering in the DA race and leaking information.

title: Firefighters ask for support for colleague critically injured in training academy fall
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Chelmsford firefighter Nick Spinale, 28, critically injured after a 40-foot fall at Massachusetts Firefighting Academy.
Spinale suffered facial fractures, severe internal damage, and wrist fractures; requires multiple surgeries and rehabilitation.
Chelmsford Firefighters Local 1839 seeks community support; acknowledges aid from Massachusetts Firefighting Academy and emergency personnel.

title: How an old Roman Anthony TikTok video inspired Red Sox’ new celebration
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox players celebrate reaching second base with moves inspired by a TikTok video featuring Roman Anthony.
Outfielder Roman Anthony's TikTok video from high school features him dancing to Mariah Carey's 'Obsessed'.
Anthony, batting .217 in 12 games, has three extra-base hits and two RBIs as Boston starts the season 4-8.

title: Former Mass. police chief charged with committing fraud while leading department
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Former Adams Police Chief Kevin Scott Kelley indicted for fraud, allegedly claiming $20,000 in unearned overtime.
Kelley faces charges of procurement fraud, false claims, and larceny over $1200 by a single scheme.
Investigation revealed discrepancies with municipal traffic enforcement grant claims; arraignment set for May 4.

title: North Andover police unions call for ‘immediate removal’ of chief after Fitzsimmons acquittal
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: North Andover police unions demand Chief Charles Gray's removal after officer Kelsey Fitzsimmons's acquittal.
Unions criticize Chief Gray's leadership, lack of body cameras, and unaddressed policies impacting officers' safety.
Fitzsimmons plans to file a civil lawsuit after acquittal, citing leadership failures and lack of accountability.

title: Report: Celtics are ‘known to be interested’ in trading for Giannis Antetokounmpo
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Boston Celtics are reportedly interested in trading for Giannis Antetokounmpo, according to The Athletic's Sam Amick.
Antetokounmpo is under contract with the Milwaukee Bucks through 2028, but the Bucks may explore trading him due to team struggles.
The Celtics face financial challenges in acquiring Antetokounmpo, potentially exceeding the NBA's luxury tax with current star salaries.

title: 5 takeaways as Red Sox pull away from Brewers, 5-0, to earn first series win
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Sonny Gray pitched 6 1/3 scoreless innings to lead the Red Sox to a 5-0 win over the Brewers.
The Red Sox secured their first series victory of the season, improving their record to 4-8.
Tyler Samaniego made a striking MLB debut, fanning three batters in a scoreless eighth inning.

title: Wu releases $4.9 billion budget proposal amid ‘difficult moment’ for city’s finances
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston Mayor Michelle Wu proposes a $4.9 billion budget for fiscal year 2027 with a 2.1% spending increase.
Budget challenges include $48.4 million deficit, rising health insurance costs, police overtime, and snow removal expenses.
Wu's proposal includes a $1.7 billion Boston Public Schools budget with hundreds of layoffs due to declining enrollment.

title: Rob Gronkowski joins Logan Mankins, Adam Vinatieri on Patriots Hall of Fame ballot
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Rob Gronkowski, Logan Mankins, and Adam Vinatieri are finalists for the Patriots Hall of Fame class of 2026.
Gronkowski is eligible for the first time, Mankins is a three-time finalist, and Vinatieri is a two-time finalist.
The decision will be made by a fan vote, with results announced after voting ends on April 28.
